steps.title: Identify the subject and what the blank requires
steps.content: The subject is “Each of these republics.” The word each is always singular regardless of the plural noun that follows. The blank is an auxiliary verb paired with the past participle “established,” and the historical context (past tense throughout) demands past tense.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the answer
steps.content:
• Choice A — have been: Plural and present-perfect — wrong number and wrong tense for a singular subject in a historical context. ✗
  • Choice B — are: Present tense, but the passage describes a historical situation (the Soviet-era republics). ✗
  • Choice C — were: Past tense but plural — “were” does not agree with the singular subject “each.” ✗
  • Choice D — was: Singular and past tense — agrees with “each” and matches the historical context of the passage. ✓
That matches Choice D — was.
